title: Initiating Printing
text: Initiating Printing You initiate the print process from within Uniface using the Proc print command or the ^Print structure editor function. Unless explicitly specified, either in parameters to the print command or in the Print dialog displayed by print/read , Uniface uses the following default values for the print mode and print job model: Print mode: All Print job model: PRINTER Figure 1. Uniface Print Dialog print and print/ask The print statement can be used anywhere in a form or report to start printing. It is executed unseen by the user and is therefore best used in reports. In forms, you can use print/ask to prompt for information, enabling the user to choose a different printer or a different layout to suit their requirements. The print/ask statement takes the supplied parameters (print job model and print mode) and presents the user with the Print form already filled in. This allows users to interactively tailor the printer specifications according to their requirements. On Windows platforms, it is also possible to use print/preview to display the Print Preview window. ^PRINT and the Print Trigger The print trigger is a component-level trigger that is activated by the ^PRINT Structure Editor function (usually mapped to a Print key). This trigger lets you control any print process initiated by the user. You can write ProcScript in this trigger which tests for certain conditions (such as the user's privileges), or, for example, run a password form component before allowing the print to continue. If the print trigger is empty (which it is by default), Uniface implicitly executes print/ask , displaying the Print dialog with the default values for the print job model and print mode.
